rulings.Case Title: EST. OF TEODORO DEL ROSARIO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/06/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Must give 15 days' notice of the status petition on the mandatory judicial council form DE-120 and file the DE-120 with the court. 2) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 3) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 3/21/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENE J. MALINOWSKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Patricia Hand Amaro failed to follow Probate Code 9252 and paid her own creditor claim without court authority. 2) Patricia Hand Amaro has not filed an allowance/rejection of the $113.61 creditor claim. Patricia Hand Amaro has not filed proof of satisfaction of the $113.61 creditor claim. 3) Patricia Hand Amaro failed to follow Probate Code 9831 and did not receive court confirmation to settle alleged claims. Patricia Hand originally characterized these payments as preliminary distributions when questioned now alleges they are settlement payments made without required court approval. 4) In the 05/20/2021 amended petition it was alleged in January 2021, Petitioner erroneously asserted that there was no property or cash remaining in the Estate. Petitioner has recently realized that she previously inadvertently used Estate funds to provide her current counsel a retainer for expenses related to this matter. In the second amended petition this information was removed. Unclear if current counsel did in fact receive a retainer or not and unclear how much was received. It appears on 08/15/2018 $1,000.00 was paid to Mary Mayo in attorney fees in violation of CRC 7.700. 4/21/2014 $4,250.00 was also paid to Jeffrey Ehrlich without a court order. 5) Petitioner alleges the true and correct value of the estate at Decedent's date of death 06/06/2004 was $71,098.84. Exhibit A shows an estate bank statement with $103,682.96 in the account on 03/31/2005. This account shows $1,496.61 in receipts. Unclear what happened to the $30k difference. 6) The account ends 12/31/2020 with $20,224.50 in assets. Petitioner cannot account for $12,313.71 of assets. Petitioner alleges there is only $7,910.79 available to distribute. Petitioner alleges she would be entitled to 1/3 interest in the estate equals $18,794.83 but she executed a disclaimer which passes her share to Sidney Amaro. 7) Court to determine how to proceed on the disclaimers.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HUGH ELWOOD MACBETH JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The sole purpose of the hearing is to determine why there has been no filed response to the Examiner notes. Second hearing for the same issue. a. Please explain why the petition alleges Decedent died September 12, 2019. The petition for probate and Order for Probate state the Decedent died September 14, 2019. b. A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. [LRSF 14.35]. c. There is no attached detailed schedules of receipts. 1 total amount for all the receipts is not detailed and must be amended. When an account has been waived, the report must contain the information required by rule 7.550. If the report is accompanied by a request for statutory commissions or fees, the basis for their computation must be included in the petition substantially in the form provided in (a). Notwithstanding the waiver of account, if the petition and report requests statutory commissions or fees based on any amount other than the amount of the Inventory and Appraisal, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705]. d. Please explain why the calculation of statutory fees allege $102.10 in additional interest accrued when the account alleges $90.86 in interest. e. Please explain the calculation of statutory fees. The Final I&A alleges $318,591.62. The account alleges $90.86 in interest. However, the calculation alleges $102.10 in interest. Please explain how $319,693.72 is the basis for the calculations of statutory compensation. f. The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c). [LRSF 14.36 I. 2)] g. Please explain who High E. Macbeth III is and why High E. Macbeth III listed as an heir of Decedent on Exhibit C and person entitled to notice on Exhibit D. h. The prayer on page 11 of the petition does not explain how the estate is to be distributed. The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. i. The proposed order must match what is requested in the petition. The proposed order page 5 #4 is not in the prayer of the petition.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FLOYD FOSTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The administrator's attorney requests $3,750 for his extraordinary services to the estate. Such services claimed to be extraordinary include 6.0 hours spent on "legal research" and preparation of an ex parte application for preliminary distribution. The filing of the ex parte application was rejected by the clerk, as the Court's local rules did not allow preliminary distribution on an ex parte basis. 2) According to the attorney's supporting time records, the attorney billed $19,386.39 for ordinary services and $6,450 for extraordinary services. The attorney's statutory compensation for ordinary services is $26,804.09, exceeding the total amount of the attorney's bills for both ordinary and extraordinary services. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate is set for 3/14/2023. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH L. TOOMEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although a Judicial Council form must not be used to supplement a pleading. [CRC 7.104(b)(2).] The "Supplemental" Spousal Property Petition filed on 2/10/22, furthermore, was not (newly) signed or verified by the petitioner; however, since a separate "Declaration" was filed contemporaneously, providing much of the same information as in the defective "Supplemental" Spousal Property Petition, the latter document will be disregarded. (The latter document, in addition, referenced only the surviving spouse's one-half interest on both Attachment 7a and Attachment 7b.) No courtesy copy of the order will be returned to Counsel, as no SASE was provided.
